title: Question?
body:
Question for the medical professionals on here

So I was sitting thinking about what's actually in vape juice while taking a hit and my chest starts to hurt in a spot that if I take a drink of water it stops so I'm wondering and I looked it up there's saline in it, so that got me wondering would it be possible, to ina way flush someone's lungs with a gently heated saline solution engineered so that's made to help dissolve it better and it can be easily rinsed out with water and like a machine to oxygenate the blood and filter it through? maybe to be done after a few days of not drinking water as to dry the lungs to better help the diluting of the chemicals that can crystallize And dried with a medical hair dryer 🤣 that doesn't have a heat function, perhaps a hot Steam while upside down instead?, since the structure of a lung under a microscope is sponge like I think that might work and that might be what popcorn lung is a crystallization of the chemicals in the lungs and that's why it hurts? Also that's how snots produced in the body right, the lungs slowly works all the gunk out and the way the Lungs takes the o2 out is embeding it in to the saliva produced in its sponge like shape with the way it collects the oxygen would it just swell from the chemicals maybe And it might just struggle to work it out because of how dense it is?
